content.text:
Based on the zoomed view of atom C:6, I can clearly identify its neighbor atoms. The carbon atom (C:6) is covalently bonded to three atoms:

## Neighbor Atoms of C:6:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|-------------|---------|
| 5 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 7 | O (Oxygen) |
| 8 | C (Carbon) |

The molecule appears to be an amide structure where C:6 is a carbonyl carbon (double-bonded to O:7, single-bonded to N:5 and C:8).

## Geomconf Index: **8894**

(This is derived from the filename `geomconf_8894.xyz`)

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 6, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
